focus: Ghana tax-benefit surface: national income tax (PAYE and self-assessment), personal reliefs, social contributions, and cash-transfer / social-protection programmes.

national.description: Ghana is a unitary state. All encoded statutes, regulations, and agency/programme policy surfaces live under the single national gh/ namespace; there is no regional layer.

purpose: Machine-readable intake map from Ghana tax-benefit tracks to upstream official source families, comparison references, and first RuleSpec encoding batches.
source_priority: Ghana Publishing Company Limited (Assembly Press) official printed Acts of Parliament and Legislative/Executive Instruments — the authentic text of Act 896 and its amending Acts.
Ghana Revenue Authority (GRA) rate tables, PAYE guides, and practice notes only after the governing Act or Instrument is identified.
Ministry of Gender, Children and Social Protection (MoGCSP) / LEAP Management Secretariat official programme documentation for social-transfer rules that are set administratively rather than by Act.
Pinned executable household-level comparison references (GHAMOD) for parity and regression tests; aggregate simulators and documentation are supporting benchmarks only.

current_blockers.description: GHAMOD (SOUTHMOD Ghana, UNU-WIDER) is the intended household-level oracle but its model-and-data bundle must clear UNU-WIDER SOUTHMOD licensing before it can be pinned as a comparison surface. Parity tests are deferred until then; see data/oracles/oracle-index.json.

Official source text for Act 896 (Ghana Publishing / Assembly Press) and the current GRA individual/PAYE rate surface and LEAP documentation must be captured into axiom-corpus under the gh/ citation-path prefix, with signed manifests, before RuleSpec modules can cite corpus provisions and encoding begins.

tracks.primary_source: Income Tax Act 2015 (Act 896), First Schedule (rates of income tax) as amended by the annual Income Tax (Amendment) Acts.
tracks.agency_surface: GRA individual income-tax / PAYE annual and monthly rate tables.

tracks.primary_source: Income Tax Act 2015 (Act 896), reliefs provisions (personal reliefs — e.g. marriage/responsibility, child education, aged dependant, disability, old age, professional/technical training) and Fifth Schedule as amended.
tracks.agency_surface: GRA reliefs guidance and practice notes.

tracks.primary_source: Livelihood Empowerment Against Poverty (LEAP) programme — MoGCSP / LEAP Management Secretariat official operations manual and gazetted grant amounts.
